opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I took the new antibiotics yesterday, and tonight my arms are covered in these little blisters. I'm terrified my skin is going to peel off.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: The blisters came up suddenly this evening after starting the new tablets and I'm frightened it's a serious allergic reaction.

symptom_narrative.duration: roughly 6 hours and I keep noticing more
symptom_narrative.character: lots of little fluid-filled blisters, some grouped in tiny rings or circles
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the blisters are small and mostly just a bit itchy, but I'm very scared about what they mean
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: on both forearms, on the outer side
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: started as an itch and then the blisters appeared over a few hours
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing really; a cool cloth eases the itch a little
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: I haven't noticed anything making it worse
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: just some mild itching around the blisters
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I can still do everything, I'm just frightened

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: a minor skin/soft-tissue infection that the antibiotics were prescribed for

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: a new oral antibiotic started two days ago

allergies_as_reported.substance: no known drug allergies before this

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives independently and manages day-to-day activities without help

objective_data_offered.value: lots of tiny clear fluid-filled blisters on both forearms, most about the size of a pinhead to a match-head, and some are grouped in little rings
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: described/shown by the patient over chat; the fine detail (whether they are tense, the exact size and pattern) cannot be confirmed without an in-person examination

objective_data_offered.value: the blisters are only on my forearms; the skin doesn't come away in sheets when I rub it and I don't see raw areas
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: patient's own lay observation, not a clinician-performed skin examination
